items.title: Guardiola turns down Italy manager job
items.summary: Italy have failed to qualify for last three World Cups Guardiola is out of work while Ancelotti is still with Brazil The Italian football federation (FIGC) has talked to Pep Guardiola , the former Manchester City coach, and Carlo Ancelotti, the Brazil manager, about taking over the crisis-hit national team. It hopes to announce the new Italy coach within the week, with the Azzurri still reeling after failing for a third straight time to qualify for the World Cup .

items.title: Klopp starts Germany reign by threatening to quit
items.summary: Jurgen Klopp is a legend at his former clubs, but he will need to convince his critics if he is to succeed in his toughest managerial challenge yet

items.title: Newcastle seal £34m move for Monaco's Bamba
items.summary: 20-year-old agrees five-year contract at club Bamba expected to compete with Miley for No 6 role Newcastle have made Aladji Bamba their fourth signing of the summer after the 20-year-old midfielder completed a move from Monaco for a fee that could rise to £34m. “I’m very happy to be here,” said Bamba. “I was very excited when I heard Newcastle were interested in signing me.

items.title: Chelsea agree deal to sign Palace defender Lacroix
items.summary: Chelsea have not paid a transfer fee for a player aged 26 or over since the summer of 2022 with the ill-fated acquisitions of Raheem Sterling and Kalidou Koulibaly. But Crystal Palace’s Maxence Lacroix is a player worth relaxing the policy for

items.title: Norway consider complaint over Balogun World Cup ban suspension
items.summary: Norwegian federation president: Fifa on a ‘slippery slope’ Trump called Infantino for review of Balogun’s red Donald Trump’s role in clearing Folarin Balogun from a mandatory one-game ban at the World Cup is set to provoke a formal complaint to Fifa ethics investigators. The Norway soccer federation said Thursday its board of directors will consider filing the complaint at its next meeting, likely on 6 August.

items.title: Football Daily | Manchester United and the quest to make mythical midfield a reality
items.summary: Real Madrid and Fulham players on club’s radar United have already signed two midfielders this summer Manchester United have identified Real Madrid’s Eduardo Camavinga and Fulham’s Sander Berge as midfield signings to further strengthen Michael Carrick’s squad. United have already signed two midfielders this window: Andrey Santos in a £48m deal from Chelsea and Youri Tielemans for £35m from Aston Villa . But with Casemiro having departed and Manuel Ugarte ruled out for what may be a prolonged period due to a knee ligament injury, the club is considering moving for another midfielder.

items.title: Andoni Iraola backs backroom staff to help cure Liverpool’s set-piece ills
items.summary: Tommy Elphick and Shaun Cooper to work on set-pieces Iraola felt ‘very lucky’ to work with them at Bournemouth Andoni Iraola has outlined the roles his backroom staff will play in trying to improve Liverpool’s set-piece record next season and admitted he was “very lucky” to inherit a talented coaching team at Bournemouth. Iraola has brought several members of his Bournemouth backroom team with him to Liverpool since succeeding Arne Slot last month . Tommy Elphick, a boyhood Liverpool fan, and Shaun Cooper have joined as first-team coaches while Iraola’s long-time colleague Pablo de la Torre has arrived as assistant coach.

items.title: Chelsea paid Aston Villa £117m for Morgan Rogers. Is he the right fit?
items.summary: The England attacker thrives when he can gallop into space but Xabi Alonso’s best team built attacks slowly By Opta Analyst It quickly became apparent this summer that Aston Villa would not be holding on to Morgan Rogers. Having helped the club finish fourth in the Premier League and win the Europa League , the 23-year-old was in demand. Arsenal looked like his likeliest next destination, with the champions eager to strengthen the left side of their attack.

items.title: Elliot Anderson lauds City as ‘kings of Manchester’ after £116m Forest transfer
items.summary: ‘They’re winners, relentless, a team I want to be part of’ Midfielder hails De Bruyne as a childhood inspiration Elliot Anderson has called Manchester City the “kings of Manchester” after completing his £116m move to the club from Nottingham Forest. The 23-year-old was a key part of England’s third-place finish at the World Cup, having been an ever-present for Forest in the Premier League last season. Before Morgan Rogers’ £117m transfer to Chelsea from Aston Villa on Tuesday , Anderson’s fee was the record for a British player.

items.title: Arsenal sign Greece forward Christos Tzolis from Club Brugge for £34m
items.summary: Tzolis joins Hincapié and Meslier among arrivals ‘⁠It’s absolutely amazing to sign for such a big ​club’ Arsenal have completed the £34m signing of Christos Tzolis from Club Brugge. The Greece international, 24, has signed a five-year contract with the Premier League champions and arrives as a replacement for Leandro Trossard following the Belgian’s switch to Besiktas . Tzolis’s move to the Emirates Stadium – Arsenal’s third permanent transfer of the summer – arrives 48 hours after the north Londoners missed out on Morgan Rogers who signed a £117m British-record fee with Chelsea, describing his new employers as the “biggest club in London” .

items.title: Trossard, Ramos, Dumfries: 10 transfers you may have missed during the World Cup
items.summary: Too engrossed with the World Cup drama to keep up with goings-on in the transfer market? We’ve got you covered Announced just before England’s soul-sucking semi-final defeat by Argentina , it would have been easy to miss Trossard’s £15.3m move to Istanbul. While, after three and a half seasons and at the age of 31, it feels like his time at Arsenal has come to a natural conclusion, he will be remembered as a player who scored in clutch moments and, ultimately, played a crucial role in the club’s first league title in 22 years .

items.title: Football Daily | Here are 48 reasons to cherish the World Cup (minus the Geopolitics)
items.summary: The Geopolitics World Cup is already fading from view, replaced by a thin July gruel of pre-season friendlies and transfer tales. But there’s still time to cast another look back over the 48-team, 104-game, 308-goal leviathan that has dominated the last six weeks of our lives. Without further ado, here are 48 reasons to cherish this year’s World Cup (minus the Geopolitics).

items.title: ‘Where did they go?’: homeless people feel force of America’s brutality in World Cup clean-up
items.summary: Fifa says football brings the world together but the unhoused in Atlanta feel targeted and completely excluded from the tournament “A lot of our community has been pushed out by the World Cup. We’re not just dollar signs, we’re more than that. We’re people and we’re frustrated that they’ve chosen to treat us less than human.” “ They dropped me off there in the middle of the night.

Med tabelläget som det är börjar tiden rinna ut – och just nu känns en degradering till division 1 mer sannolik än ett nytt kontrakt i Superettan

items.title: Police call on English football clubs to ease security burden on taxpayers
items.summary: Home Office reveals police incidents for 2025-26 season Four arrests for every 100,000 people attending games The number of football matches in England and Wales featuring incidents reported to police reached record levels last season, even as the number of arrests continued to return to pre-Covid figures. According to an annual report by the Home Office, released on Thursday, there was a 4% increase in the number of matches with reports of “football-related anti-social behaviour, violence and disorder” during the 2025-26 season. Reports were made at 1,644 games, up from 1,580 the previous campaign.

items.title: ‘Everyone is hooked on football’: Indian league seeks to ride World Cup wave
items.summary: Club chiefs hope that a revitalised setup can help huge interest in this summer’s tournament translate into growth of the Indian domestic game India was not at the World Cup, but the tournament drew huge interest from within the country . This has happened before but now comes with significant, and necessary, changes in the domestic game that could transform a quadrennial passion into perennial interest. After more than a year of negative headlines regarding the Indian Super League, the country’s top tier will kick off on 4 September.
